_meta.title: PediaDose — Drug Library
sources: KKH CE Clinical Guidelines, January 2026 edition (Revised 4 Mar 2026)
NHG Pharmacy Paediatric Dose Calculator, July 2024 (Version 2024.07.26)
PaedsENGAGE Common Paediatric Drug Reference Guide, 1st Edition February 2025
_meta.warning: For clinical decision support only. All dose values must be verified by a qualified clinician against current formulary guidelines before use with real patients.

drugs.name: Paracetamol

routes.notes: Use higher end of dose range for analgesia

formulations.label: 500 mg tablet
formulations.ageNote: For children who can swallow tablets

drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026, p.9; NHG Pharmacy Calculator Jul 2024; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Ibuprofen

routes.notes: Lower range for antipyretic effect; higher range for anti-inflammatory effect

drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026, p.9; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Morphine Sulphate
drugs.frequency: PRN; repeat dose at 1/2 initial dose, max 0.1 mg/kg

routes.notes: Administer slowly over 3–5 minutes. Inform senior doctor before use.

cautions: Inform senior doctor before any patient receives parenteral morphine
Use with caution in asthma or pre-existing respiratory compromise
Use with caution in obstructive sleep apnea — halve the dose
Monitor vital signs post-administration until conscious level returns to baseline
Antidote: Naloxone 0.01 mg/kg (max 0.4 mg) IV, may repeat every 2–3 min

drugs.name: Oseltamivir
cautions: Needs dose adjustment in renal impairment

drugs.name: Acyclovir
drugs.dosingNote: VZV (chickenpox): start within 24h of rash, treat for 5 days. HSV gingivostomatitis: same dose.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin — standard dose
drugs.notes: Standard dose for most infections. Use high-dose regimen for AOM, CAP, sinusitis.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin — high dose
drugs.notes: High-dose regimen for AOM, community-acquired pneumonia, sinusitis. 80–90 mg/kg/day given as BD for compliance.
drugs.source: PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025; KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026

drugs.name: Co-amoxiclav
drugs.notes: Dose based on amoxicillin component. Standard: 25 mg/kg/dose BD. High-dose (AOM/CAP/sinusitis): 40–45 mg/kg/dose BD.
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Cephalexin
drugs.notes: Split total daily dose to 3 divided doses for compliance if needed. Severe: 15–25 mg/kg QDS
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026, p.346; NHG Pharmacy Calculator Jul 2024; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Cefuroxime
drugs.notes: Suspension and tablets are NOT interchangeable. Tablet dose for >12yo: 250–500mg BD

drugs.name: Azithromycin
drugs.dosingNote: 3-day course shown. 5-day alternative: 10 mg/kg OD day 1, then 5 mg/kg OD days 2–5.

drugs.name: Clarithromycin
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; NHG Pharmacy Calculator Jul 2024; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Co-trimoxazole (Trimethoprim/Sulfamethoxazole)
cautions: Contraindicated in G6PD deficiency (risk of haemolysis)
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; NHG Pharmacy Calculator Jul 2024; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Clindamycin
drugs.dosingNote: Mild infection dose shown (5–7 mg/kg/dose TDS). Moderate/severe: 10 mg/kg/dose TDS (7–13 mg/kg/dose), max 600 mg/dose.
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
cautions: Not routinely first-line. Use only after assessment of risks and benefits. Risks of tendinopathy in children.

drugs.name: Ondansetron
cautions: Single dose only — QT prolongation risk. KKH CE ED protocol.
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025

drugs.name: Prednisolone

indications.notes: Start as early as possible — takes at least 4 hours to act

drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026, p.163–173; Paeds_Meds_v2; PRD Phase 2

drugs.name: Salbutamol

ageBandedDosing.notes: 2–6 puffs via spacer and mask
0.5 mL 0.5% salbutamol + 2.5 mL 0.9% NaCl
1 mL 0.5% salbutamol + 2 mL 0.9% NaCl

drugs.dosingNote: Each MDI puff delivers 100 mcg. Minimum nebuliser volume 0.5 mL. For acute severe asthma protocol, search 'Asthma' by condition.

drugs.name: Albendazole

ageBandedDosing.notes: Single dose pre-meal
Single dose pre-meal

drugs.treatmentDuration: Single dose; may repeat for 3–5 days depending on indication

drugs.name: Dexamethasone
drugs.source: PaedsENGAGE Feb 2025; KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026

drugs.name: Cetirizine
drugs.dosingNote: Age-based reference (KKH CE): 6m–<2yr: 2.5 mg OD; 2–6yr: 2.5–5 mg OD; 6–12yr: 5–10 mg OD; >12yr: 10 mg OD. Weight-based calculation should align with these bands.
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PRD Phase 2

drugs.name: Lorazepam
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PRD Phase 2

drugs.name: Diazepam
drugs.source: KKH CE Guidelines Jan 2026; PRD Phase 2

drugs.name: Adrenaline (Epinephrine)
drugs.frequency: Stat IM; repeat every 15 min if required
drugs.dosingNote: EpiPen banding: 7.5–25 kg → EpiPen Jr 0.15 mg IM; >25 kg → EpiPen 0.3 mg IM. Nebulised for croup (0.5 mL/kg of 1:1000, max 4 mL) — search by condition.
cautions: Do not give IV in anaphylaxis

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 × 100 mg sachet BD
2 × 100 mg sachets BD
1 × 600 mg effervescent tablet BD

drugs.name: Chlorpheniramine
cautions: Use with caution in children 1–2 years

drugs.name: Famotidine
drugs.dosingNote: Infants <3 months: 0.5–1 mg/kg OD (once daily). Dose shown is for ≥3 months.

ageBandedDosing.notes: 30 mg/5 mL suspension — 2.5 mL BD. Proven for urticaria only.
30 mg/5 mL suspension — 5 mL BD
60 mg tablet BD or 180 mg tablet OD

drugs.name: Hydroxyzine
drugs.dosingNote: Max dose by age: <6yr → 12.5 mg/dose; ≥6yr → 25 mg/dose.

drugs.name: Lactulose
drugs.dosingNote: PaedsENGAGE weight-based alternative: 0.5 mL/kg every 12–24H. Titrate to effect.

ageBandedDosing.notes: Max 3 days
Max 5 days
5 mg/5 mL syrup — 5 mL OD
10 mg tablet OD
Loading: 500 mg, then 250 mg every 6H PRN. Max 3 days.

drugs.name: Oxymetazoline (Iliadin)

ageBandedDosing.notes: Use Normal Saline drops instead — do not use oxymetazoline
0.01% nasal drops — 1 drop each nostril TDS
0.025% nasal drops — 1 drop each nostril TDS
0.05% nasal drops/spray — 1 drop/spray each nostril TDS

drugs.treatmentDuration: ≤5 days — do not exceed to avoid rebound congestion
cautions: Do not use in infants <1 month — use saline instead

drugs.name: Tramadol
drugs.dosingNote: Max 50 mg/dose for patients <50 kg. Max daily dose 400 mg.
cautions: Reduce to max 50 mg/dose if patient <50 kg

ageBandedDosing.notes: 4 mg granules sachet or chewable tablet
5 mg chewable tablet
10 mg film-coated tablet

drugs.name: Erythromycin
cautions: Risk of hypertrophic pyloric stenosis in infants <6 weeks

drugs.name: Nystatin

ageBandedDosing.notes: 100,000 units (1 mL) QDS — apply to each side of mouth after feeds
100,000–500,000 units (1–5 mL) QDS — rinse and swallow

drugs.dosingNote: Oral drops: 100,000 units/mL. Continue for 48h after symptoms resolve.

drugs.name: Ipratropium bromide

ageBandedDosing.notes: 250 mcg per dose via nebuliser
500 mcg per dose via nebuliser

drugs.dosingNote: Give with salbutamol in acute severe asthma. Frequency: Q20min for first 3 doses, then Q4–6H.

drugs.name: Budesonide (nebulised)

ageBandedDosing.notes: 2 mg single dose — use 2× 1 mg/2 mL nebules or 4× 0.5 mg/2 mL nebules

drugs.dosingNote: For moderate-severe croup. May repeat once after 12h if symptoms recur. Alternative to oral dexamethasone.

drugs.name: Hydrocortisone (IV)

ageBandedDosing.notes: 2–4 mg/kg IV (max 200–250 mg) — single dose
4 mg/kg IV (max 100 mg) — Q6H
2 mg/kg IV/IM (max 100 mg) — stat, then Q6H

cautions: Hospital use only — IV/IM administration required

drugs.name: Chloramphenicol eye drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 drop per affected eye TDS–QDS; up to 2-hourly in severe infections. Continue 48h after healing. Suitable for infants.

drugs.dosingNote: 0.5% eye drops. Also available as 1% ointment — apply TDS or at bedtime alongside drops.

drugs.name: Fusidic acid eye drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 drop per affected eye BD

drugs.dosingNote: 1% viscous eye drops (Fucithalmic). BD dosing is sufficient due to viscous formulation. Usual course: 7 days.

drugs.name: Fluticasone furoate nasal spray (Avamys)

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1–2 sprays per nostril once daily

drugs.dosingNote: 27.5 mcg per spray. Use in children ≥2 years. Onset may take 3–7 days — use regularly for best effect.

drugs.name: Mupirocin ointment

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply small amount to affected area TDS for 5–7 days

drugs.dosingNote: 2% ointment (Bactroban). Cover with dressing if needed. Discontinue if no improvement after 3–5 days.

drugs.name: Clotrimazole cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply to affected area BD–TDS

drugs.dosingNote: 1% cream (Canesten). Duration: 2–4 weeks for tinea; continue for 2 weeks after clearing. Active against Candida and dermatophytes.

drugs.name: Permethrin

ageBandedDosing.notes: 5% lotion — apply over whole body including face, neck, scalp and ears. Wash off after 8–14h. Repeat after 7 days.

drugs.dosingNote: Reapply if hands washed with soap within 8h of application. Treat all household contacts simultaneously. Wash clothing and bedding after treatment.

drugs.name: Calamine lotion

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply to affected areas OD–QDS. May reapply after bath.

drugs.dosingNote: Anti-pruritic. Cooling and soothing. Shake before use. Used for chickenpox, insect bites, and pruritic rashes.

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin + Hydrocortisone ear drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 3 drops into affected ear BD. Duration: 7 days. Consider 1 drop in smaller children with smaller ear canal volume.

drugs.dosingNote: Ciprofloxacin 2 mg/mL + Hydrocortisone 10 mg/mL ear drops. For otitis media with perforation — apply topically. Not for systemic infection.

drugs.name: Mometasone nasal spray

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1–2 sprays per nostril once daily. May increase to BD in acute rhinosinusitis (adults).

drugs.dosingNote: 50 mcg per spray. Use in children ≥2 years. Onset may take 3–7 days — use regularly for best effect.

drugs.name: Tobramycin eye drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 drop BD–QDS. Severe infection: 1 drop every 4H. Max 2 drops every hour in severe infection until improvement.

drugs.dosingNote: 0.3% eye drops (Tobrex). Can be used in neonates (practice based on labelled indications). Continue 48h after resolution.

drugs.name: Hypromellose eye drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1–2 drops into affected eye(s) 1–2 hourly PRN for symptom relief

drugs.dosingNote: 0.3% eye drops. Lubricating drops for viral conjunctivitis symptom relief. No antibacterial activity — do not use for bacterial conjunctivitis.

drugs.name: Chlortetracycline 1% eye ointment

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply thin strip (~1 cm) to conjunctiva TDS
1 application PRN

drugs.dosingNote: 1% eye ointment. For conjunctivitis: apply thin strip to lower conjunctival sac. Also used topically for facial/superficial wounds.

drugs.name: Olopatadine eye drops

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 drop into affected eye(s) BD–QDS

drugs.dosingNote: Olopatadine HCl 0.1% (Patanol). For allergic conjunctivitis. Use in children ≥3 years.

drugs.name: Zinc oxide ointment

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application PRN to affected area

drugs.dosingNote: 40% zinc oxide (Desitin). Protective barrier cream for regular diaper rash/dermatitis. Apply at each nappy change.

drugs.name: Miconazole nitrate 2% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application BD, or OD when used together with miconazole powder. Duration: 2–6 weeks.

drugs.dosingNote: Continue for 1 week after symptoms disappear. For fungal diaper rash.

drugs.name: Daktacort cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application once to twice daily for 3–4 days until redness improves, then switch to antifungal monotherapy

drugs.dosingNote: Hydrocortisone 1% + Miconazole nitrate 2% (Daktacort). Steroid and antifungal combination. Short course only — switch to antifungal alone once inflammation settles.

drugs.name: Diclofenac sodium 1% gel

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply to affected area TDS–QDS

drugs.dosingNote: 1% gel. For topical analgesia. Use in children ≥1 year.

drugs.name: Ketoprofen 30 mg plaster

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply 1 plaster BD to affected area

drugs.dosingNote: Kefentech 30 mg adhesive plaster. Avoid pasting on the same skin area repeatedly to prevent irritation.

drugs.name: QV cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply liberally PRN

drugs.dosingNote: Humectant and moisturiser. For dry skin, eczema, and dermatitis. Apply after bathing while skin is still damp.

drugs.name: Emulsifying ointment

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply liberally PRN

drugs.dosingNote: Emollient base. Used as soap substitute and moisturiser. For eczema and dry skin. Apply in direction of hair growth to reduce folliculitis.

drugs.name: Hydrocortisone 1% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply thinly to affected areas OD–BD

drugs.dosingNote: For mild eczema. 0.5% strength available for babies and young infants. Mild-potency steroid (Class VI–VII). Avoid face, skin folds, and occluded areas unless directed.

drugs.name: Soragel

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application to affected oral mucosa PRN

drugs.dosingNote: Cetalkonium Cl 0.01% + choline salicylate 8.714%. For oral ulcers and teething (≥4 months, as stated on packaging).

drugs.name: Oracort E dental paste

ageBandedDosing.ageLabel: All ages (generally avoided in young children)
ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application to affected oral mucosa TDS PRN. Press gently into lesion — do not rub.

drugs.dosingNote: Triamcinolone acetonide 0.1% + Lignocaine HCl 3% dental paste (Oracort E). Generally avoided in young children. Contains Lignocaine — do not use excessively.

drugs.name: Benzydamine lozenges

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 lozenge every 1–2h PRN. Max 12 lozenges per day.

drugs.dosingNote: Each lozenge contains benzydamine 3 mg (sugar-free). Difflam. For sore throat and oral ulcers. Use in children ≥6 years.

drugs.name: Dequalinium lozenges

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 lozenge every 2–3h PRN. Max 8 lozenges per day.

drugs.dosingNote: Each lozenge contains dequalinium 0.25 mg. For sore throat. Use in children ≥12 years only.

drugs.name: Acyclovir 5% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply to affected area 5 times a day for 5–10 days

drugs.dosingNote: 5% cream (Zovirax). For herpes labialis (cold sores). Start at first sign of symptoms. Duration: 5–10 days.

drugs.name: Malathion 0.5% lotion

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply over whole body including scalp, neck, face and ears. Wash off after 24h. Repeat application after 7 days. Reapply if hands washed with soap within 24h.
Apply sufficient amount on dry hair to thoroughly wet hair and scalp. Allow hair to dry naturally. Wash off after 8–12h. Repeat application after 7–9 days if needed.

drugs.dosingNote: Use fine-toothed (nit) comb after head lice treatment to remove dead lice and eggs.

drugs.name: Chlorhexidine gluconate 1% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: Clean affected area and apply generously PRN

drugs.dosingNote: 1% cream. Antiseptic for first-degree and superficial burns, wounds, and abrasions.

drugs.name: Tetracycline 3% ointment

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application PRN to affected area

drugs.dosingNote: 3% ointment. For superficial abrasions and wounds. Avoid application to facial region in children <4 years.

drugs.name: Fusidic acid 2% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application BD–TDS for 7 days

drugs.dosingNote: 2% cream (Fucidin). For infected abrasions and superficial skin infections. Duration: 7 days.

drugs.name: Fusidic acid 2% + Betamethasone valerate 0.1% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application BD to affected area

drugs.dosingNote: For infected abrasions and inflammatory skin conditions with secondary infection. Use in children ≥1 year only. Betamethasone valerate 0.1% is a moderate-potency steroid.

drugs.name: Clotrimazole 1% + Beclomethasone 0.025% + Gentamicin 0.1% cream

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application BD–TDS to affected area

drugs.dosingNote: For infected abrasions. Contains Beclomethasone dipropionate 0.025% — a potent steroid. Use in children ≥1 year only. Short course only.

drugs.name: Octenisan wash lotion

ageBandedDosing.notes: Apply as body wash during showering or wiped over body during sponge bath. Use for 5 days.

drugs.dosingNote: Antimicrobial body wash. Used for decolonisation and wound care. Apply and leave briefly before rinsing.

drugs.name: Potassium permanganate solution

ageBandedDosing.notes: 1 application PRN. Dilute 1:8 parts with water before use.

drugs.dosingNote: 1:1000 solution, dilute 1:8 with water. May be used to compress or wash affected areas. Dab dry after use — not necessary to wash off. Stains skin and clothing.
